description_en: Existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence.
example: The artist is famous for her colorful abstract paintings.
note: Type of art that doesn't represent reality.

description_en: The assembled spectators or listeners at a public event such as a play, movie, concert, or meeting.
example: The audience cheered loudly at the end of the performance.

description_en: A work of outstanding artistry, skill, or workmanship.
example: The Mona Lisa is considered Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece.

description_en: A category of artistic composition, as in music or literature.
example: My favorite movie genre is science fiction.

description_en: An act of staging or presenting a play, concert, or other form of entertainment.
example: The singer gave a moving performance of her latest hit.

description_en: A public display of works of art or other items of interest.
example: There is a new exhibition of modern art at the city gallery.

description_en: A copy of a work of art.
example: I bought a high-quality reproduction of a Van Gogh painting.

description_en: The written text of a play, movie, or broadcast.
example: The actors spent weeks memorizing their lines from the script.

description_en: An elaborate musical composition for full orchestra, typically in four movements.
example: Beethoven's Ninth Symphony is one of the most famous pieces of classical music.

description_en: A building or outdoor area in which plays and other dramatic performances are given.
example: We are going to the theater to see a musical tonight.

description_en: A room or building for the display or sale of works of art.
example: The city art gallery is hosting a collection of local landscape paintings.
note: Space for temporary or permanent art display.

description_en: A person who expresses an unfavorable opinion of something or someone.
example: The new film received mostly positive reviews from the critics.

meaning: phát sóng; chương trình phát thanh
description_en: Transmit (a program or some information) by radio or television.
example: The news is broadcast live every evening at 6 p.m.

description_en: The use of the imagination or original ideas, especially in the production of an artistic work.
example: Visual arts are a great way for children to express their creativity.
note: Quality of being original and imaginative.

description_en: A group of musicians, actors, or dancers who perform together.
example: The jazz ensemble played until midnight.

description_en: A large group of musicians who play many different instruments together.
example: The symphony orchestra consists of over 100 musicians.
note: Includes strings, woodwinds, brass, and percussion.

description_en: The most intense, exciting, or important point of something.
example: The plot reached its climax in the final chapter of the book.

description_en: The art of making motion pictures.
example: The cinematography in that movie was absolutely breathtaking.
note: Focuses on lighting, framing, and camera movement.

description_en: An entrance hall or other open area in a building used by the public, especially a hotel or theater.
example: We met in the theater foyer before the show started.

description_en: An outstanding part of an event or period of time.
example: The fireworks were the highlight of the festival.

description_en: The action of improvising.
example: Jazz is known for its extensive use of musical improvisation.
note: Creating art or performance without preparation.

description_en: The words of a song.
example: He writes meaningful lyrics that resonate with many people.

description_en: A book, document, or piece of music written by hand rather than typed or printed.
example: The museum holds several rare medieval manuscripts.
note: Original written version of a work.

description_en: The state of being famous or well known for some bad quality or deed.
example: The artist gained notoriety for his controversial public statements.

description_en: The first performance of a musical or theatrical work or the first showing of a movie.
example: The world premiere of the movie was held in Los Angeles last night.

description_en: The main figure or one of the most prominent figures in a real situation or a literary text.
example: The story follows the journey of the protagonist as he seeks justice.

description_en: A group of four people playing music or singing together.
example: The string quartet played a piece by Mozart.

description_en: A stock of plays, dances, or pieces that a company or a performer knows or is prepared to perform.
example: The pianist has a large repertoire of classical and jazz music.
note: Collection of works a performer can do.

description_en: A person who writes scripts for movies or television.
example: She won an award for best original screenplay as a scriptwriter.
note: Writes descriptions of action and dialogue.

meaning: sự tri ân; vật phẩm tưởng niệm
description_en: An act, statement, or gift that is intended to show gratitude, respect, or admiration.
example: The concert was a tribute to the legendary singer who passed away recently.
note: Honor or respect shown to someone.

description_en: Forthcoming; about to happen.
example: Tickets for the upcoming music festival go on sale tomorrow.
note: Something planned for the near future.

description_en: A singer, typically one who performs with a jazz or pop group.
example: The band's lead vocalist has an amazing range and power.

description_en: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.
example: Minimalist architecture focuses on a clean and simple aesthetic.
note: A set of principles underlying art or style.

description_en: A book that is among those having the largest sales in a given period.
example: His latest novel reached the best-seller list within a week.

description_en: The act or process of choosing actors for a play or movie.
example: The casting for the new superhero movie took three months.

description_en: A keeper or custodian of a museum or other collection.
example: The museum curator organized the special exhibition of Egyptian artifacts.
note: Professional in charge of a collection.

description_en: A unit used to measure the intensity of a sound.
example: The concert was so loud that the sound reached 110 decibels.

description_en: A repeated or additional performance of an item at the end of a concert.
example: The audience cheered until the band came back for an encore.

description_en: The state of being known or talked about by many people, especially on account of notable achievements.
example: He achieved worldwide fame after his first movie was released.

description_en: A person who is invited to visit someone's home or attend a particular social occasion.
example: Many famous actors were invited as guests to the red carpet event.

description_en: Relating to or characterized by musical harmony.
example: The song has a beautiful harmonic structure.

description_en: A person who draws or creates pictures for magazines, books, advertising, etc.
example: The children's book was brought to life by a talented illustrator.

description_en: A type of music of black American origin characterized by improvisation, syncopation, and usually a regular or forceful rhythm.
example: Jazz clubs are very popular in this part of town.

description_en: A prevailing tone or central theme.
example: The keynote speaker at the conference was a Nobel laureate.
note: Sets the theme for an event.

description_en: An extremely famous or notorious person, especially in a particular field.
example: The late guitarist was a true rock and roll legend.

meaning: vở kịch tâm lý xã hội; phim sướt mướt
description_en: A sensational dramatic piece with exaggerated characters and exciting events intended to appeal to the emotions.
example: The film was a classic melodrama with a lot of tears and heartbreak.

description_en: The action of nominating or being nominated for an award or a position.
example: She received an Academy Award nomination for Best Actress.
note: Initial stage of becoming an award winner.

description_en: The first public performance or showing of a play, movie, or art exhibition.
example: The grand opening of the new art gallery was a success.
note: First day of an event for the public.

description_en: The main events of a play, movie, or similar work.
example: The movie has a very complex plot with many unexpected twists.
note: Sequence of events in a story.

description_en: A group of words taken from a text or speech and repeated by someone other than the original author or speaker.
example: The book starts with a famous quotation from William Shakespeare.
